entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 10 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 10 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-10 07:02AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘Opening of a new world’: How international students are helping seniors to log on
  • Tim Sheens tells Wests Tigers he’s ready to take top job
  • Experienced jumper dies in skydiving accident near Geelong
  • Why the Blues are ready for anything - and everything - to go wrong
  • China blames Coalition government for ‘difficulties’ with Australia, proposes four point plan
  • Ten years on from the God particle, we may have found a fifth force of nature
  • Sims sin-binned for shoulder to head
  • Second test day three LIVE: Sri Lanka lose just one wicket before lunch as Australia toil in the field
  • Dragons speedster burns Broncos
  • Bombers scrape home against undermanned Brisbane
  • Dutch farmers bring towns to a standstill in backlash against emissions reductions
  • Fatty blows up over Sims binning
  • Umpires 'don't know what to do' with Ginnivan
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Suspect blamed Abe for mother’s bankruptcy, used ‘simple to make’ DIY gun
  • Clean energy demand to drive copper price higher in future, despite recent falls
  • Off Country: Private schools’ tough lessons on Indigenous education
  • Quick Q: Who and what Foxtel’s content boss most admires
  • Roaming Rooster: Manu’s Turbo-themed approach to halves headache
  • ‘Young, funny, irritatingly handsome’: Euan’s challenge in taking on Joseph
  • Boris Johnson is gone, but he leaves important lessons for Liberals
  • We mourn Shinzo Abe as a friend, but the world will miss his wisdom

  • Best of cartoons, July 9, 2022

  • Destroying bees a sad necessity

  • NSW government loses bid to stop rail industrial action
  • Britain is falling apart, and it could finish the Tories
  • Pregnant woman issued with ticket says fetus is passenger after Roe v Wade ruling
  • Long queue of candidates jostle to succeed Boris Johnson
  • Sri Lankan protesters storm homes of President and PM
  • How China’s COVID-zero policy is giving economy a long-term boost

  • Sydney regions with most bus cancellations revealed

  • Victoria ambulance service declared 'code red'

  • Experts set to arrive at scene of New South Wales fatal helicopter crash today
  • I’m not religious, but my faith is beyond belief
  • Unfairly or not, a globe-trotting PM courts trouble (if only Menzies listened to his wife)
  • Australia shouldn’t blindly follow NZ wellbeing budget model
  • Despite numbers falling away, core Christianity will continue
  • Spotted! Melbourne’s water bottle girl, bringing balance to her life – and the city
  • The Elon Musk-Twitter saga now moves to the courts
  • After 50 years in Earlwood, ‘cruel’ strata bill threatens elderly couple with homelessness
  • Finally, Glass Animals are bringing the Heat Waves – and their tattooed bums
  • Dramatic Jane Eyre needs a little more conversation, a little more action
  • Taylor Hawkins' son Shane honours father with rendition of 'My Hero' by the Foo Fighters
  • Forget open mic nights, TikTok is where aspiring comedians are trying to make it
  • The Australian who designs the palaces of kings, billionaires and... Clive Palmer
  • Our film industry may be booming but we’re not telling Australian stories
  • Daisy Edgar-Jones on family, feeling like an outsider and instant fame
  • Push to let social media influencers sell sunscreen after ban on beauty ads
  • Actor Tina Bursill: ‘I have never been married, in the biblical sense’
  • National weather forecast for Sunday July 10
  • Australian species brought back from extinction in NSW after 200 years
  • ‘Tiny feet poking out’: Breeding boom for rare plains wanderer
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: NY Times: 10 Jul, 2022

entry.content:

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 10 Jul, 2022

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Taipei Times: 10 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

  • Shinzo Abe’s body arrives in Tokyo as Japan mourns

entry.title: Guardian: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live news: Zelenskiy dismisses ambassadors; six killed in strike on Donetsk apartment block
  • The Magnificent Ambersons at 80: Orson Welles’ powerful but cursed drama
  • Boris Johnson leadership signalled Tory move to party of the populist right brexit
  • Full speed ahead: the enduring appeal of the road movie
  • The ‘Thomas court’: after biding his time, rightwing justice finds his power
  • The big picture: preserving neighbourhood moments in Houston, Texas
  • Sunday with Kim Wilde: ‘If it’s sunny, I’ll start gardening in my pyjamas’
  • After the coup, the contest that will expose faultlines in a fractured Conservative party
  • Raquel Welch on the secret to staying fit in 1984
  • British foreign policy was on autopilot – Boris Johnson showed we have choices
  • I fear my parents will disapprove of my new partner
  • In brief: The Wrath to Come; Briefly, A Delicious Life; This Much Is True – reviews
  • Shinzo Abe: police admit security ‘problems’ for former PM as election vote begins
  • Kyrgios and Djokovic set dinner date side bet ahead of Wimbledon clash
  • Tory turmoil keeps Bank of England in the dark on tackling crisis
  • Labour calls for investigation into Boris Johnson’s private meeting with ex-KGB agent Alexander Lebedev
  • England’s Jill Scott making most of home Euros after long road to the top
  • Impressive Scotland level series with comfortable win in Argentina
  • Hopes US baby formula shortage could ease as key Abbott plant restarts
  • Jill Roord strikes to rescue draw for holders Netherlands against Sweden
  • Boris Johnson facing accusation he tried to get job for woman claiming affair
  • Chelsea agree £50m deal to sign Raheem Sterling from Manchester City
  • How the Ukraine invasion resurrected a photographer’s 26-year time warp to the Soviet Union’s collapse
  • ‘Like going back 1,000 years’: ancient Māori bird hunt faces uncertain future
  • ‘It’s a crisis’: US summer pools closed or cut back amid lifeguard shortage
  • Native American elders recall abuse at US government boarding schools
  • Gomes and Silva rescue Portugal to deny sluggish Switzerland
  • Revealed: officials raised ‘flag’ over Nadhim Zahawi’s tax affairs before he was appointed chancellor
  • Elena Rybakina’s victory puts Kazakhstan on map of world tennis | Simon Cambers
  • With him right until the very end, the Tory press who loved Johnson as one of their own | Nick Cohen
  • Josh Adams makes history for Wales as late try sets up first win in South Africa
  • Luis Echeverria, Mexican president of the 1970s, dies at 100
  • India seal T20 series win but Gleeson shines on England debut

  • Sri Lanka: president agrees to resign amid unrest
  • Chequers is off, what’s done cannot be undone. All that is left is to be a super trouper | Catherine Bennett
  • Mick Herron: ‘I’m interested in incompetence, things going wrong’
  • ‘Jokes are strategic’: how Mykolaiv’s leader uses humour to resist Putin
  • When it comes to banter, men are in their element. But that is no foundation for lasting friendship

  • US army general suspended for mocking Jill Biden’s support of abortion rights
  • A word of warning that Boris Johnson’s departure might not be all it seems | Alex Clark

  • Fears of ‘Trump moment’ before Boris Johnson was finally made to realise his time was up
  • Priti Patel’s plan to end Channel crossings in disarray as navy threatens to ‘walk away’
  • On my radar: Rosie Holt’s cultural highlights
  • Thor: Love and Thunder review – sentimental multimillion-dollar romp
  • At least 16 killed as flash floods hit Hindu pilgrimage in Kashmir
  • ‘We lost our fear’: the Basque terror group’s killing that made Spain say enough is enough

  • Relief for England after Billy Vunipola and Owen Farrell sink Australia

  • Blinken voices concern to China over stance on Russia’s war in Ukraine
  • Pearl Jam review – a sensitive, subversive new vision for classic rock
  • My body dilemma: ‘What are my breasts for? Why have I been carting them around for years?’
  • Johnson loyalists hit back at ‘treacherous’ Sunak as leadership race begins
  • ‘Disturbing’: weedkiller ingredient tied to cancer found in 80% of US urine samples
